completed will make one of the
incest halls in the city. The union
will also occupy the offices front
ing on Riverside avenue. 1t is prob.
able that several of the unions now
meeting at the bartenders' hall,
corner Wall and Main, will move to
the new hall at the same time a.
the bartenders. Henry N. Oertor,
secretary of the bartenders' or
ganization, says the union never
was so prosperous as at present.
The funds belonging to the union
in the banks now amount to near.
ly $500 and the membership is
more than 300.


The bartenders union will OBJ
serve Decoration day by decorat-
ing the graves of all deceased
members Of the union.


Seven girl chocolate dippers at
the Washington Cracker Factory
have gone on strike. In the win
ter the girls are paid by the week
but during the summer it is GUS
tomary to pay them so much q
pound for every pound of candy
they dip.


When the piece work scale was
to be inaugurated this summer the
girls asked that they be paid
cents pound. The management
agreed to pay them only IN cents
pound and the strike resulted.
At ~ cents a pound the girls
state that they could only make
from 81 to SO a week.


They are usually paid from S. to
81 week.
